contextBanner: [TBG | Local Artifact Engine | harness/control-plane | branch: feat/local-artifact-engine-control-plane]
purpose: Parse repo-local and explicitly supplied local artifacts, normalize evidence, cascade registered read-only engines, and produce operator-facing English reports without treating raw artifacts as self-executing truth.

modes.off: The harness records no automatic artifact action. A manual run remains available.
modes.observe: The harness parses artifacts and writes reports, but it does not cascade registered action engines.
modes.auto: The harness parses changed artifacts and cascades every eligible read-only downstream engine.
modes.strict: The harness performs auto processing and returns a nonzero result when required inputs are missing or parsing produces blockers.

philosophy: Artifacts are evidence-bearing events, not inert attachments and not unquestioned truth.
The registry owns engine identity, dependencies, trigger edges, input candidates, and output names.
The local state file owns operator authority. Prompts and individual producer scripts do not silently override the toggle.
Every engine action must be deterministic, idempotent, local, bounded, and read-only with respect to source code, Git history, pull requests, worktrees, saves, and game state.
An upstream engine may enqueue downstream engines only through declared registry edges.
A cascade must reject cycles, enforce a maximum engine count, and exclude its own output directory from discovery.
JSON owns variables and machine state. English reports own operator explanation. The two surfaces must describe the same terminal state and next decision.
Artifact parsing may classify proof, but it may not manufacture proof that the source artifact does not contain.
The automatic path must fail closed when strict mode encounters missing required inputs, invalid JSON, a registry cycle, or an unregistered engine.
allowedScope: read explicitly supplied additional artifact roots
parse JSON, JSONL, Markdown, text, and log files within configured size limits
write ignored normalized packets, reports, events, progress, fingerprints, state, and handoff files
start or stop a repo-local watcher process when the operator changes the toggle
classify terminal state, proof boundary, blockers, and exact next commands
forbiddenScope: edit tracked source files during engine execution
execute git reset, clean, merge, rebase, cherry-pick, push, branch deletion, or worktree removal
open, close, retarget, merge, or comment on pull requests
execute arbitrary commands read from an artifact
claim build, launcher, behavior, or runtime proof from parser success

requires: the registry and workflow contract parse
the operator can query, enable, disable, and toggle automatic processing
manual run works while automatic processing is disabled
observe mode parses but does not cascade downstream engines
auto mode cascades declared downstream engines
strict mode fails on a malformed required artifact
repo hygiene output produces a repo-floor context packet
stale PR recovery output produces a bounded next-action packet
the handoff engine consumes upstream engine packets
the JSON result, JSONL events, progress log, English report, and handoff agree
